title: With Just That, I Feel Like I Could Keep on Running Forever
overview: Tsukamoto Tsukushi is invited by his classmate Kazama Jin to play futsal because their team is lacking a player.

title: I Want to Be Part of the Dream
overview: Tsukushi and Kazama, along with all the other first years who joined the soccer club, are forced to run outer laps as a collective punishment.

title: Playing Soccer With You Is Super Fun, You Idiot
overview: On the first day of joint training camp, Tsukushi overhears the students from other schools talk about Kazama's middle school days.

title: I Want to Put My Life on the Line to Live
overview: Coach Nakazawa carefully observes everybody at practice as he thinks about the line-up for the Inter-high tournament.

title: That's Why I Will Keep on Running, Kazama-kun
overview: Tsukushi helps with the training of third-year Kasahara, who decided to quit after the Interhigh; the line-up for the Interhigh is announced.

title: Running for Someone: That's the Kind of Soccer I Want to Play
overview: Indou Kaoru, the captain of Seiseki's rival school, Saku High, appears before Tsukushi and invites him to play soccer.

title: I Wish I Could Repay My Debt, If But a Little
overview: The second preliminary match of the Inter High is Seiseki versus Minami High School, and thanks to Kazama's plays, Seiseki wins with a huge lead.

title: If He Gets Into It, He Will Become a Bullet
overview: In the finals, the Seiseki soccer team will be facing off against the current Kantou Champion, Sakuragi High School.

title: We'll Get the Ball Over to Where Your Voice Can Reach Us in No Time
overview: Mizuki is fully recovered and Seiseki's eleven is in top shape to fight; however, Tsukushi has concerns about Kazama, who is acting different than usual.

title: I Won't Be Defeated by Such a Tiny Setback
overview: After Mizuki takes the lead for Seiseki, they put Kazama on Indou Kaoru; however, just five minutes later, Indou's shot lands in the net.

title: In Order to Connect These Gleaming Days to the Future
overview: Tsukushi enters the game to replace Ooshiba, who has exhausted all his energy; as Tsukushi tries to step onto the field, his shorts fall down and his underwear is exposed.

title: I Won't Let Anyone Be Disappointed Again
overview: The team is shaken, especially Tsukushi and Kazama, who both feel something they've never felt before.

title: You Just Need to Look Forward
overview: Tsukushi's childhood friend Sayuri asks him to take a break and go to the summer festival with her; a few days later, the members of the Seiseki soccer team gather at Ooshiba's place.

title: Give Everything to Take My Spot Away From Me
overview: As soon as the summer vacation starts, Seiseki's soccer club is off to the five day Sakurajima joint training camp.

title: I'm Sure I Can't Stay at Seiseki Any Longer
overview: The training match ends on a bad note for Tsukushi, and in the evening, he joins third-year Haibara to review the recording of the match.

title: The Endless Waves That Keep Crashing In
overview: After talking to Kazama about the dilemma of wanting to be a regular, but not wanting to fight his friends for that spot, Tsukushi tries to find a solution.

title: I Love This Team
overview: On the final day of the camp, Seiseki is playing against Seikan High School; right off the bat, Taira's bullet-like pass is caught by the super rookie, Himura, and the ball ends in Seiseki's goal.

title: Tell Me, Kimishita: You Can Do It, Right?
overview: Tsukushi is swapped to replace the injured Kazama; meanwhile, on the Seikan bench, the coach and Taira are arguing about the best way to utilize Himura.

title: Come With Me: It's a Straight Shot to Nationals
overview: Right as the match was about to end, Tsukushi puts everything into his shot and secures the victory for Seiseki.

title: Don't Underestimate Seiseki
overview: Seiseki steadily advances through the preliminaries for the championship in the Tokyo area, and their next opponent is Hokutou High School.

title: Isn't Soccer a Blast?
overview: Even though Seiseki is missing it's Three Arrows, Kimishita and the other third-years put on a strong show.

title: Right Now, I'm Seiseki's Captain
overview: Due to Nitobe's red card, Seiseki is down to ten players, and to make matters even worse, Keiou's top two players, Kai and Maruoka put pressure on Seiseki's goal.

title: I'm a Member of Seiseki's Soccer Team, Too
overview: After causing an upset, Seiseki commits to defense, but Keiou's attacks are relentless; then, in the final three minutes, just as Usui is about to clear the ball, something unforeseen happens.

title: I Want to Play More Soccer With This Team
overview: Even without the Three Arrows, Seiseki was able to put on a performance worthy of their reputation, but Tsukushi is glum.
